a:r.a:t: The API surface tells you the scope of what this system exposes. On the public side, 48 endpoints follow the OpenAI-compatible protocol, meaning any client that speaks the OpenAI API can connect without modification. The chat endpoints include /v1/chat/completions for standard chat and /v1/connector/chat for MCP-integrated conversations. Document endpoints handle upload, ingestion, and source downloads. Graph endpoints let you list, get, index, import, and query knowledge graphs. Skills endpoints provide full CRUD plus activate, deactivate, preview, and rollback. MCP connection endpoints handle CRUD, tool listing, testing, and refresh. Jobs and tasks endpoints manage background work, and the team mailbox endpoints let agents communicate through channels. On the admin side, roughly 78 endpoints power the control panel. Agent management has 5 endpoints for listing, getting, updating, deleting, and hot-reloading agents. Collection management has 13 endpoints for CRUD, document listing, upload, sync, repair, and health checks. The access control section has 15 endpoints covering principals, roles, bindings, memberships, and permissions — this is the RBAC system we will detail later. Graph admin has 15 endpoints for full graph lifecycle management including build, refresh, validate, prompt tuning, and skill assignment. Config admin provides 4 endpoints for schema inspection, effective config view, validation, and runtime apply. All admin endpoints require X-Admin-Token authentication.
